audit_log.check: Autonomy: PASS (scaling frameworks adapt to spend level, not rigid templates)
Consciousness: PASS (RIGHT-SMART systematic scaling + BOTTOM-SAFE risk management)

dependency.notes: Pack System and M3 Lanes foundational for scaling
ncROAS validation required before budget increases

component.activation_strategy: RIGHT systematic progression (3-level roadmap) + BOTTOM protective gates (IF ncROAS <1.5x → Don't scale)

framework.name: The 3-Level Scaling System
framework.key_quote: "Level three is the most advanced part... we are introducing cost caps."
framework.summary: Progressive account structure evolving as spend increases from validation to scale.

level.name: Validation
level.focus: Creative finding and product-market fit validation

key_actions.action: Test 3:2:2 Flexible Ads to find initial winners
Build 180-day Purchaser audience for future exclusions

level.name: Growth
level.structure: Introduce "The Pack System" (New Ad Sets for new creative batches) + Add Retargeting
level.focus: New Customer spend prioritization and systematic testing

key_actions.action: Launch new Pack each week with dated naming: "Pack 2026-01-19"
Implement M3 Swim Lanes (Prospecting 60-70%, Retargeting 20-30%, Retention 5-10%)
Use Ad Set Spending Limits to force testing of new packs
Begin Post ID Harvesting to scaling campaign

level.name: Scale
level.structure: Cost Caps, Value Rules, Day Parting introduced
level.focus: Efficiency optimization and incremental lift validation
level.budget_strategy: Cost per Result Goal (Cost Caps) for CPA stability at high volume

key_actions.action: Switch to Cost Caps: Set bid = Target CPA + 20% buffer
Implement Value Rules for high-LTV segments
Geographic expansion using Value Rules (Tier 1 bid 100%, Tier 2 bid -20%)
Incremental Attribution as primary reporting metric

framework.name: The Pack System (Horizontal Scaling)
framework.key_quote: "Every time you launch new creatives you launch a new pack... creating a hyper competitive environment."
framework.summary: Launch new Ad Set ("Pack") for every creative batch within Prospecting CBO; CBO auto-reallocates to best pack.

steps.step: Create 3-5 new creatives (distinct concepts, not variations)
Launch new Ad Set in Prospecting CBO: "Broad Pack 2026-01-19"
Meta reallocates budget automatically to best-performing pack
Old packs naturally fatigue and get less spend; new packs tested
Only pause packs with Frequency >4.0 + rising CPA for 3+ days

framework.name: Budget Cliff Detection & Recovery
framework.summary: Using "Ad Set Spending Limits" to force algorithm to test new scaling candidates when "Spend Hog" blocks growth.

steps.step: Identify new scaling candidate getting 0 spend
Set "Ad Set Daily Minimum" to 1x Target CPA (e.g., $50)
Run for max 7 days
Recovery: IF performs, remove limit (scales naturally). IF fails, pause

framework.application: When one pack dominates 90%+ of CBO budget, blocking new tests

framework.name: Post ID Harvesting (Vertical Scaling)
framework.reference: See KB-CAMPAIGN-ARCHITECTURE F4 for full framework
framework.summary: Move winning ad from test to scale environment by copying Post ID to preserve social proof (10-30% CPM reduction).

framework.name: Value Rules Geographic Expansion
framework.key_quote: "Group together more and more countries in the exact same campaign and downgrade the bid."
framework.summary: Scale internationally without fragmenting account by using Value Rules to bid lower for lower-value markets within single campaign.

steps.step: Group Tier 1 (US/UK) and Tier 2 (EU/AU) in one Ad Set
Create Value Rule: "If Location is NOT US, Decrease Bid by 20%"
Allows volume from Tier 2 without destroying US blended ROAS

framework.when_to_use: Scale phase ($30k+/month); US market saturated or high CPMs

framework.name: Cost Cap Implementation (Level 3)
framework.key_quote: "Spend is variable... allowing cost per acquisition to stay more flat."
framework.summary: Switch from "Highest Volume" to "Cost per Result Goal" to maintain efficiency at high scale.
framework.mechanism: You set price (CPA); Meta controls volume. Spend drops on bad days, skyrockets on good days

implementation.step: Change Bid Strategy to "Cost Per Result Goal"
Set Budget to 5-10x Daily Spend (high budget, capped by bid)

framework.name: Incremental Attribution Validation
framework.summary: Check "Incremental" attribution column to verify scaling ad drives new customers vs retargeting.
framework.rule: IF "Incremental Purchases" >70% of "7-day Click Purchases" → Ad is scalable. IF <50% → Retargeting fluff

category.name: Diagnostic
category.heuristic: IF Platform ROAS >3.0 but MER declining THEN "Attribution Hallucination" (Over-crediting retargeting) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 822]
IF new Pack gets zero spend in CBO THEN not necessarily failure; algorithm prefers existing winner. Use Ad Set Limits to force test [SRC: Sam Piliero | 770]
IF Frequency >3.5 in Prospecting THEN hit audience ceiling with that creative angle [SRC: Tier 11 | 724]
IF Spend high but New Customer % <40% THEN "Broad" targeting collapsed into retargeting audience [SRC: Sam Piliero | 512]

category.name: Optimization
category.heuristic: IF ncROAS >1.5x THEN increase CBO budget by 20% [SRC: Sam Piliero | 700]
IF using Cost Caps and spend stops THEN increase bid cap by 10-20% to re-enter auction [SRC: Sam Piliero | 521]
IF creative wins in testing THEN do not turn off; replicate to Scaling Campaign using Post ID [SRC: Professor Charley T | 103]
IF spending >$10k/mo THEN ensure launching at least 1 new creative concept per week [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]

category.name: Scaling
category.heuristic: IF scaling internationally THEN do NOT create separate campaigns per country; use Value Rules to consolidate [SRC: Sam Piliero | 830]
IF scaling winner THEN add banner (e.g., "Black Friday") to existing creative to refresh without losing learning [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 884]
IF hit "Budget Cliff" (efficiency drops at higher spend) THEN introduce 2nd prospecting campaign with totally different creative angle [SRC: Matt Shiver | 57]

category.name: Troubleshooting
category.heuristic: IF scaling leads to "Junk Leads" THEN optimize for deeper funnel event (e.g., "Booked Call" instead of "Lead") [SRC: Caden Thompson | 296]
IF one ad hogs 90% of CBO spend THEN launch separate "Testing Campaign" to force budget to new concepts [SRC: Ben Heath | 610]
IF cost caps not spending THEN switch back to "Highest Volume" with lower budget to rebuild signal [SRC: Sam Piliero | 519]

steps.step: Check ncROAS: Is New Customer ROAS >Target?
IF YES: Increase Prospecting CBO Budget by 20%
Check "Packs": Any old packs fatigued (Freq >3.0, CPA rising)? Pause them
Launch New: Deploy 1 new "Pack" (Creative Batch) regardless of performance (Rule of 1:10k)

sop.name: Rule of 1:10k Production Cycle
sop.rule: For every $10,000 spend per month, upload that many ads per week
sop.action: Ensure creative pipeline matches spend velocity to prevent "Creative Starvation"

steps.step: Launch new Ad Set
Set "Daily Minimum Spend" = 1x CPA Target
Set calendar reminder for 7 days
Day 7: Remove limit. If hasn't scaled naturally by then, it's a loser
Identify Target CPA (e.g., $50)
Duplicate winning Ad Set
Change Bid Strategy to "Cost Per Result Goal"
Set Bid = Target CPA + 20% (e.g., $60)
Set Budget to 5-10x Daily Spend (High budget, capped by bid)

knowledge_block.metrics_table: <metric name="Frequency" purpose="Ceiling detection" threshold="<3.5 (Prospecting)" action_trigger="Launch New Concept Pack" source="[SRC: Tier 11 | 724]"/>

failure_mode.name: The Budget Cliff
failure_mode.root_cause: Algorithm exhausted "Easy" audience for current creative angle
failure_mode.recovery: Launch new "Concept" (not variation) in new Pack to unlock new psychographic segment

failure_mode.name: Creative Starvation
failure_mode.symptoms: Performance slowly degrades over weeks; inability to scale
failure_mode.root_cause: Failing Rule of 1:10k (Not enough new creatives to support spend)
failure_mode.recovery: Immediate "Creative Sprint" to batch 5-10 new static concepts using Nano Banana

failure_mode.name: Attribution Hallucination
failure_mode.symptoms: Platform ROAS 4.0 but bank account empty (MER 1.5)
failure_mode.recovery: Switch to "Incremental Attribution" view; cut Retargeting budget

failure_mode.name: The Spend Hog
failure_mode.symptoms: One ad gets 90% of budget; new tests get 0%
failure_mode.root_cause: CBO predictive modeling favoring history over exploration
failure_mode.recovery: Apply "Ad Set Spending Limit" (Min = 1x CPA) for 7 days

golden_run.name: The Messy Middle Breakout ($10k → $50k)
golden_run.action: Implement Pack System. Launch 1 new Pack per week
golden_run.result: Spend diversifies across 4 active packs. ncROAS stabilizes

golden_run.name: High-Volume Stability (Cost Caps)
golden_run.context: Spend $3k/day, highly volatile CPA ($40 one day, $120 next)
golden_run.action: Switch to Cost Caps (Bid = $60 CPA). Set Budget to $10k/day (Liquid)
golden_run.result: Spend fluctuates ($2k-$8k) but CPA holds steady at $55

golden_run.name: International Scale
golden_run.action: Add UK/AU/CA to Prospecting CBO. Use Value Rules to bid -10% on non-US
golden_run.result: CPM drops to $35; blended ROAS increases due to cheaper inventory

compliance_guardrails.guardrail: Rule of 1:10k: Do not scale spend without scaling creative production. $50k spend = 5 new ads/week minimum [SRC: Sam Piliero | 772]
Incremental Truth: Never report blended ROAS as "Performance." Use ncROAS or Incremental ROAS [SRC: Sam Piliero | 832]
Exclusions: Always exclude 180-day purchasers from Prospecting to ensure accurate ncROAS [SRC: Sam Piliero | 761]
Budget scaling: Maximum 20% increase every 2-3 days to avoid resetting learning phase [SRC: Chase Chappell | 253]

checklist.name: Weekly Scaling Checklist
checklist.item: [ ] Calculate ncROAS (New Rev / Spend) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 822]
[ ] Is creative pipeline meeting Rule of 1:10k? [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]
[ ] Are any "Packs" hogging spend without results? (Apply Limits) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 770]

checklist.name: Budget Adjustment Protocol
checklist.item: [ ] If ncROAS <Break-even: -20% Budget or Pause Losers [SRC: Chase Chappell | 871]
[ ] If using Cost Caps: Increase Bid 10% to force more volume [SRC: Sam Piliero | 521]
